title: leucaena greggii
intro: Leucaena greggii, also known as the Desert Davana or Guajillo, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States, Mexico, and parts of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-cancer, and cardiovascular support. Leucaena greggii is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its potential to support digestive health and immune system function.
